summary: Verified crossOrigin removal from AudioPlayer.jsx. Audio is STILL BROKEN: all 4 audio players show MediaError code 4 (MEDIA_ERR_SRC_NOT_SUPPORTED). Root cause is NOT crossOrigin - files download successfully (HTTP 200/206) but Chromium refuses to decode. Likely MIME type issue: CDN returns 'audio/x-m4a' which Chromium may not accept; standard is 'audio/mp4'. Images verified still working (4/4 on homepage).
passed: AudioPlayer.jsx: crossOrigin attribute correctly removed (verified in code and DOM: crossOrigin=None)
Voices page loads with 4 audio elements in DOM
Audio files are network-accessible: fetch HEAD returns 200, content-type: audio/x-m4a, content-length ~11MB
Audio file requests are made: all 4 m4a URLs return HTTP 200/206

failed.issue: All 4 audio elements fail to load with MediaError code 4 (MEDIA_ERR_SRC_NOT_SUPPORTED). readyState=0, networkState=3, duration=NaN. Error message: 'The element has no supported sources.' Fix from iteration_7 (removing crossOrigin) did NOT resolve issue. Confirmed reproducible even with fresh `new Audio()` element using the same src - so bug is not in AudioPlayer component logic.
failed.evidence: Audio 0-3 all: readyState=0, networkState=3, error={code:4, message:''}. Fetch HEAD succeeds (200, content-type: audio/x-m4a, 11.1MB). Manual `new Audio(); a.src=src` also fires 'error' with code 4. Webpack dev overlay shows 'ERROR: The element has no supported sources.'

action_items: Root cause is likely MIME type: CDN serves .m4a files with 'audio/x-m4a', but Chromium expects 'audio/mp4' or 'audio/mpeg' to reliably decode M4A. Try one of:
1) Add explicit <source src={audioUrl} type='audio/mp4' /> inside <audio> instead of src attribute - this forces browser to try mp4 codec
2) Verify the M4A files are actually valid AAC-in-MP4 containers (not raw AAC or corrupted). Test one file locally with ffprobe.
3) If files are valid, consider re-uploading with content-type override to 'audio/mp4', or proxy through backend to rewrite Content-Type header.
4) Do NOT restore crossOrigin - that was correctly removed.
